purpose: The master agent's highest-priority runtime obligation: scan ALL remaining tasks across ALL waves on every worker completion and dispatch every task whose dependencies are satisfied. Defines the 5-step dispatch mechanism, server-side automatic dependency tracking via tasks_unblocked SSE events, the /api/dashboards/:id/dispatchable endpoint, and the four core parallelization principles. Loaded continuously throughout swarm execution.
gotchas: Waves are PURELY VISUAL — they have ZERO bearing on dispatch logic. Never wait for a wave to finish before dispatching next-wave tasks. If you removed the 'wave' field, dispatch logic should not change at all.
On every worker completion, scan the ENTIRE agents[] array — not just the next wave. A completed task may unblock tasks in waves 3, 5, AND 7 simultaneously; all must dispatch in the same pass.
Failed tasks NEVER enter the completed set — they do NOT satisfy dependencies. Only status==='completed' counts. Treating a failed task as completed cascades broken output downstream.
Always populate UPSTREAM RESULTS with KEY DETAILS (function signatures, export names, file locations) — without it the downstream worker re-reads files redundantly or makes wrong assumptions.

Don't forget to rewire depends_on arrays when creating a repair task — every reference to the failed task ID must be replaced with the repair task ID, otherwise downstream tasks point at a never-completing ID.
Server-side tasks_unblocked SSE alerts (with 100ms DEPENDENCY_CHECK_DELAY_MS) are a complement, NOT a replacement — manual eager dispatch (Steps 1-5) remains the authoritative mechanism.
The Task tool caps simultaneous tool calls at ~8-10 per message — for larger waves, batch into back-to-back dispatch rounds but NEVER wait for the first batch to complete before sending the second.
patterns: 5-step dispatch: build completed set from progress files (status==='completed') -> build in_progress set -> iterate agents[] checking (id not completed) AND (id not in_progress) AND (every depends_on entry IS in completed set) -> dispatch ALL passing tasks in parallel -> log each dispatch.

Server's automatic dependency tracking: fs.watch detects completion -> 100ms settle delay -> DependencyService.computeNewlyUnblocked() -> broadcasts tasks_unblocked SSE event with {dashboardId, completedTaskId, unblocked[]}.
GET /api/dashboards/:id/dispatchable returns all tasks with every dependency completed and no progress file (still pending).
Repair task dispatch flow: log failure -> create repair agent in initialization.json -> rewire depends_on -> dispatch repair worker with failed_task.md protocol -> run eager dispatch scan as normal.
conventions: Principle 1 — Always parallelize independent work. Sequential execution of independent tasks is a failure mode.
Principle 2 — Dependency-driven dispatch, not wave-driven. Waves are visual, dependencies are constraint.
Principle 3 — Pipeline must flow continuously. Record completion -> immediate scan -> dispatch every unblocked task in same cycle.
Principle 4 — No artificial concurrency cap. Send as many agents as there are ready tasks. Bottleneck should be dependencies, not arbitrary limits.
Repair task ID convention: '{failed_wave}.{next_index}r' (e.g., 2.4r for repair of failed task in wave 2 with 3 existing tasks).
